item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images used in this article are cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image comes from here and the second image comes from here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I saw this very cute video of a Japanese Emperor caterpillar walking around on someone's hand and I found it relevant: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uoBDnpb-95I

i like its :3 face and the little feets

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was actually afraid this would end unresolved when I realized they were trying to find each other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, and I will! Even in my limited time on the site I have noticed that. I suppose that around sieries III users began to expand their horizons beyond the realm of internet horror and experiment with other genres, testing their compatibility both the universe and clinical nature of the website.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you like SCPs that are more weird and light-hearted than spooky or gory, you should definitely keep reading SCPs between 2000 and 2999. There are still death demons of doom in Series III, but there's lots and lots of just about everything else too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As someone who hates romance litirecture and can't stomach J-Pop… I really thought this was something special. Givin the grit and gore that most SCP articals put you through, this was rather refreshing. Really just a light read of something good hearted, in between your regularly scheduled SCP-666 "Death Demons Of Doom"s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As someone in a long-distance relationship, this really strikes home for me. It's impressive that you were able to make such a nuanced story within the limits of the challenge, and in not very many words, too!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I respect these opinions, I would like to point out that I feel that all the languages it uses are justified. The books that they eat come from America, they also show up in Japan, and the French, well, the bugs do love each other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not getting the feels everyone else seems to be feeling. I'm pretty tired of articles with unrelated "it also makes english text" effects, and the text itself seemed pretty generic. I also found the last addenda to be very cheesy. Overall, the initial premise interested me but I was disappointed by the last paragraph onward.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those are some cute little bugs. Tells a story quite well. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're actually not J-pop lyrics, it's just that the skip had to be based on those lines. Also, thanks for catching the bad syllables, fixed now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fictional location, just pick a random rural area in Montana.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nice - upvoted.

I see now that the quotes are all J-pop lyrics. When reading, I initially wondered whether the first quote was intended as a haiku (in English), and felt the absence of two additional syllables in the first line - can I convince you to change it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't find a place called Barnswood in Montana

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The feels…
the feeels!
THE FEEEEEELS!
(hihahaba then died)

THE END

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These was a heartwarming read. I enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was not prepared for this level of feels. Excellent job!

item.title: Say it with me; "Daaawwwww!"
item.content:encoded:
Posted in response to Zyn's bug/J-pop challenge. Zyn is best zyn there is for posting that challenge and looking this over.

Long story short:
I had to write a love story using those two pics and the following Japanese pop song lines as themes.
"My overflowing words in the present can't reach you" from Milk
"Go forth tonight. Don't inform anyone before you start feeling comfortable" from Re: Dreamer
